questions.explanation: Grasshoppers feed by chewing the leaves of plants, unlike butterflies (which feed on nectar as adults), spiders (predators), and ticks (parasites that suck blood).

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the animals below is not an invertebrate?
questions.explanation: A fish has a backbone, making it a vertebrate, unlike a bee, cockroach, and grasshopper which are invertebrates (insects without backbones).

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the list below carefully.
1 Has three body parts
2 Its body is divided into two parts
3 Has six legs
4 Has eight legs
Which numbers describe the basic structure of a spider?
questions.explanation: A spider's body is divided into two parts (cephalothorax and abdomen) and it has eight legs, unlike insects which have three body parts and six legs.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the diagram of a fruit shown below.

The type of seed dispersal of the fruit shown above is...
questions.explanation: The diagram shows a twisted, pod-like fruit, which is typical of plants that disperse seeds by explosion (the pod dries and splits open suddenly, flinging seeds out).

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following drugs can cause damage to a person's body if taken for years?
questions.explanation: Cocaine and mandrax are illicit drugs that cause long-term body damage when abused, unlike cocoa, fancida (an antimalarial), and panadol which are not illicit harmful drugs.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the list below.
1 Pubic hair grows
2 Morning sickness
3 Appearance of pimples
4 Enlargement of breasts
5 Breaking of the voice
The numbers showing features of pregnancy are...
questions.explanation: Morning sickness and enlargement of breasts are features associated with pregnancy, while pubic hair growth, pimples, and voice breaking are features of puberty, not pregnancy.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: What movements of the earth cause seasons, days and nights?

options.A: Seasons: Revolution, Day and Night: Rotation
options.B: Seasons: Rotation, Day and Night: Revolution
options.C: Seasons: Revolution, Day and Night: Revolution
options.D: Seasons: Rotation, Day and Night: Rotation

questions.explanation: The Earth's revolution around the sun causes seasons, while its rotation on its own axis causes day and night.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following substances can ruin the lives of people if abused?
questions.explanation: Dagga (marijuana) is an illicit substance that can ruin people's lives if abused, unlike bruffen, caffeine, and panadol which are common, legal substances.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the procedures below is followed in the extraction and purification of copper in Zambia?
questions.explanation: The correct sequence in copper extraction and purification is: crushing, floating (floatation/concentration), smelting, and then electrolysis to refine the copper.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following methods of communication uses sound?
questions.explanation: Mobile phones and drums both use sound to communicate (voice calls and drum beats), unlike e-mail, fax, and letters which are visual/written forms of communication.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following are items made from copper?
questions.explanation: Copper is commonly used to make coins and jewellery due to its malleability and attractive appearance, unlike permanent magnets, hoes, roofing sheets, and vehicle batteries which are typically made from other materials.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
The diagram below shows a small box.

(Diagram shows dimensions: 4cm height, 2cm width, 5cm length)

What is the volume of the box?
questions.explanation: Volume = length × width × height = 5cm × 2cm × 4cm = 40cm³.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
During an experiment, learners used solid materials as shown in the diagram below.

(Diagram shows rods 1: iron rod, 2: wooden rod, 3: copper rod, 4: glass rod, each with wax attached and heated by a candle)

In which of the rods above will the wax melt?
questions.explanation: Iron and copper are good conductors of heat, so heat travels along these rods and melts the wax, unlike wood and glass which are poor conductors (insulators) of heat.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: A person who cannot stop abusing drugs is...
questions.explanation: A person who cannot stop abusing drugs is described as an addict, reflecting a dependency on the substance.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the list of fertilizers below.
1 Dead plants
2 Green manure
3 Animal waste
4 Compound D
Which of the fertilizers is not organic?
questions.explanation: Compound D is a chemical (inorganic) fertiliser, unlike dead plants, green manure, and animal waste, which are all organic fertilisers derived from living or once-living material.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the pie chart below showing composition of air.

(Pie chart shows sections labeled G, H, F, and E, with E being the largest section)

The gas represented by the part marked E is...
questions.explanation: Nitrogen makes up the largest percentage of air (about 78%), corresponding to the largest section E in the pie chart.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: What do we call animals which destroy certain crops by feeding on them?
questions.explanation: Animals that destroy crops by feeding on them are called pests.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following consists of properties of copper?
questions.explanation: Copper is a good conductor of electricity and is resistant to rust (corrosion), making 'conductivity and resistant' the correct pairing of its properties.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: The normal human body temperature is...
questions.explanation: The normal human body temperature is approximately 37°C.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is an insulator?
questions.explanation: Plastic is an insulator, meaning it does not conduct electricity, unlike aluminium, copper, and silver which are good conductors of electricity.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Where in the alimentary canal are nutrients and water absorbed?

options.B: Nutrients: Small intestine, Water: Large intestine

questions.explanation: Nutrients are absorbed in the small intestine, while water is mainly absorbed in the large intestine.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: A Grade 7 learner wanted to obtain salt from a mixture of salt and sand. What process did the learner use to achieve this?
questions.explanation: To separate salt from a mixture of salt and sand, the mixture is first dissolved in water and filtered to remove the sand, then the salt solution is evaporated to recover the salt.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following methods can produce a high pitch of sound?

options.C: Hitting a filled up container of water

questions.explanation: Hitting a metal bar produces a high pitch of sound due to its high frequency vibrations, unlike the other actions which produce lower or duller sounds.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is a simple machine used to hold things together?
questions.explanation: A screw is a simple machine used to hold things together, unlike an inclined plane, pulley, and seesaw which serve other functions such as lifting and moving objects.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: One of the advantages of chemical fertilizer in agriculture is that it...

options.B: improves the water retention of the soil.
options.D: supplies nutrients to plants over a longer period.

questions.explanation: Chemical fertilisers supply nutrients to plants quickly, allowing for fast nutrient uptake, unlike organic fertilisers which release nutrients more slowly.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is a list of non metals?
questions.explanation: Carbon, hydrogen, oxygen, and carbon dioxide are all non-metals, unlike the other options which include aluminium and/or zinc, which are metals.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the diagram of the alimentary canal below.

(Diagram shows parts labeled 1, 2, 3, 4 - esophagus, liver area, large intestine, small intestine)

In which of the labelled parts does absorption of food take place?
questions.explanation: Part 4 in the diagram represents the small intestine, where most absorption of digested food takes place.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: What is the advantage of keeping livestock together?

options.A: Diseases are more likely to spread
options.B: Diseases are not likely to spread

questions.explanation: Keeping livestock together makes it easier for farmers to manage, monitor, and care for them, despite the increased risk of disease spread, which is a separate disadvantage.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is not a way of retaining water in the soil?
questions.explanation: Spraying is not a method of retaining water in the soil; mulching, shading, and terracing all help retain soil moisture by reducing evaporation or runoff.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Food which is not digested in the alimentary canal is...
questions.explanation: Undigested food material that passes through the alimentary canal is eventually eliminated from the body as faeces.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is a source of light in the solar system?
questions.explanation: The Sun is the source of light in the solar system; Earth, Mars, and the Moon only reflect light from the Sun.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the following lists.
1 Iron, Zinc, Copper, Aluminium
2 Carbon, Hydrogen, Zinc, Carbon dioxide
3 Iron, Zinc, Hydrogen, Oxygen
4 Carbon dioxide, Copper, Zinc, Oxygen
Which list consists of metals only?
questions.explanation: Iron, zinc, copper, and aluminium are all metals, unlike the other lists which include non-metals such as carbon, hydrogen, oxygen, or carbon dioxide.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following are skin diseases?
questions.explanation: Measles, scabies, and chicken pox are all diseases that affect the skin, unlike the other options which mix in unrelated conditions such as scurvy (deficiency disease) and marasmus (malnutrition).

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is the best way of looking after HIV and AIDS patients?

options.A: Give them good nutrition and love
options.B: Let them use their own cups
options.D: Support but isolate them from the community

questions.explanation: Giving HIV and AIDS patients good nutrition and love is the best way to care for them, unlike isolating them or restricting their rights, which causes unnecessary stigma and harm.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Many cooking pots are made of metals instead of clay because a metal is...
questions.explanation: Metal is a better conductor of heat than clay, allowing pots to heat up quickly and evenly for cooking.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following are needs of livestock?
questions.explanation: Food and water are essential basic needs of livestock, unlike the other pairings which are not both essential needs in the same way.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
What is the volume of the following object?

(Diagram shows dimensions: 4cm, 2cm, 2cm)
questions.explanation: Volume = length × width × height = 4cm × 2cm × 2cm = 16cm³.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: One of the methods of conserving animal species from becoming extinct is by ...

options.A: keeping the animals in game parks.
options.D: killing some animals for a period of time.

questions.explanation: Keeping animals in game parks protects them from poaching and habitat loss, helping to conserve animal species from becoming extinct.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: What do doctors use to determine the body temperature of patients?
questions.explanation: A thermometer is the instrument doctors use to measure the body temperature of patients.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Excessive use of alcohol harms the body by causing...
questions.explanation: Excessive alcohol consumption is well known to cause liver damage.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is not a ventilator?
questions.explanation: A plain roof is not a ventilator; air vents, doors, and windows allow air to circulate in and out of a building, unlike a solid roof.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the following diagram.

(Bar graph showing amount of rainfall in cm³ for Mon-Sun: Mon 5, Tue 10, Wed 5, Thu 9.5, Fri 3, Sat 5, Sun 15)

On which day was the lowest rainfall recorded?
questions.explanation: Friday recorded the lowest rainfall amount (approximately 3 cm³) compared to all other days shown in the bar graph.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: In the water cycle, what is the change from liquid to gas state called?
questions.explanation: Evaporation is the change from liquid to gas state in the water cycle.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Why are ventilators important in a building?
questions.explanation: Ventilators are important in a building because they allow free air circulation, ensuring fresh air movement and removal of stale air.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: The property of Copper that makes it useful all over the world is its ...
questions.explanation: Copper's good conductivity (of electricity and heat) is the property that makes it useful worldwide, particularly in electrical applications.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is not a deficiency disease?
questions.explanation: Dysentery is an infectious disease caused by bacteria or parasites, not a nutritional deficiency disease, unlike kwashiorkor, marasmus, and rickets which result from nutrient deficiencies.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the following diagram.

(Diagram shows a measuring cylinder with scale 1-5)

What does the above apparatus measure?
questions.explanation: The apparatus shown is a measuring cylinder, which is used to measure volume.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Farmers are advised not to crowd their animals because ...
questions.explanation: Crowding animals increases the risk of diseases spreading easily among them, which is why farmers are advised against it.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following organs is not part of the digestive system?
questions.explanation: The kidneys are part of the excretory system, not the digestive system, unlike the large intestine, pancreas, and small intestine which are all digestive organs.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Grade 5 learners left plant materials in a pit to decompose in order to make ...
questions.explanation: Decomposing plant materials in a pit produces compost manure, an organic fertiliser.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the following list of food types.
1 Pumpkin
2 Mango
3 Pawpaw
4 Carrot
The list contains food rich in...
questions.explanation: Pumpkin, mango, pawpaw, and carrot are all fruits/vegetables rich in vitamins.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: A farmer keeping cattle cleaned the kraal twice a week in order to prevent ...

options.C: bad smell of animal urine and dung.
options.D: dust from settling in the kraal.

questions.explanation: Cleaning the kraal regularly primarily helps prevent the outbreak of diseases among the cattle by maintaining hygiene.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is the difference between organic and inorganic fertilizers?

options.A: Organic: Made from natural material, Inorganic: Made from chemicals
options.B: Organic: Sold as granules in bags, Inorganic: Sold as manure
options.C: Organic: Very expensive, Inorganic: Very cheap
options.D: Organic: Small amounts required, Inorganic: Large amounts must be used

questions.explanation: Organic fertilisers are made from natural materials (such as plant or animal matter), while inorganic fertilisers are made from chemicals, which is the correct distinguishing characteristic.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Communication is important because it...
questions.explanation: Communication is important because it helps to pass messages between people.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
The following diagram shows a simple machine.

(Diagram shows scissors with parts labeled 1, 2, 3, 4)

Which numbered part represents the pivot?
questions.explanation: Part 2 in the scissors diagram represents the pivot point, the fixed point around which the blades rotate.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following animals is classified as a mammal?
questions.explanation: A cow is classified as a mammal, unlike a duck (bird), frog (amphibian), and lizard (reptile).

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the following experiment showing a property of matter.

(Diagram shows a bottle submerged in a dish of water with bubbles rising)

The experiment shows that air ...
questions.explanation: Bubbles of air escaping from the inverted bottle as water enters demonstrate that air occupies space, since the air had to be displaced for water to fill the bottle.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Some children who were left with a brazier inside a poorly ventilated room suffocated but were saved by quickly ...

options.A: blowing air on them using a fan.
options.C: taking them out for fresh air.

questions.explanation: Taking suffocating children out of a poorly ventilated room into fresh air is the immediate, life-saving action needed to address oxygen deprivation/carbon monoxide poisoning.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Dust from quarrying and mine dumps affects the environment by causing ... pollution.
questions.explanation: Dust from quarrying and mine dumps causes air pollution (dust particles in the air) and land pollution (dust settling on land), unlike the other pairings which include noise or water, which are not the direct effects of dust specifically.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Sound can be made louder by...
questions.explanation: Making vibrations stronger increases the amplitude of sound waves, making the sound louder.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the following diagram of the digestive system.

What happens in the part labelled X?
questions.explanation: Part X in the diagram represents the small intestine, where absorption of digested food (nutrients) takes place.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the following diagram.

(Diagram A shows a Eureka can with a stone submerged in water on a string, overflowing into B, a measuring cylinder showing 10cm³ of displaced water)

What is the volume of the stone?
questions.explanation: The volume of water displaced and collected in the measuring cylinder (10cm³) equals the volume of the stone, by the principle of water displacement.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Learners carried out an investigation to find out whether the following substances were conductors or insulators.
- Plastic
- Rubber
- Paper
The above were discovered that they were ...
questions.explanation: Plastic, rubber, and paper are all bad conductors (insulators) of electricity.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following plants is propagated using suckers?
questions.explanation: Bananas are propagated using suckers, which grow from the base of the parent plant, unlike potatoes (tubers), maize (seeds), and cassava (stem cuttings).

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is an alternative energy source in view of load shedding?
questions.explanation: Solar energy is a practical and commonly adopted alternative energy source for individual households and businesses to address load shedding (since hydro is typically the main grid source affected by load shedding itself).

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
The following diagram shows an animal.

(Diagram shows a lizard-like animal)

The animal above is a ...
questions.explanation: The lizard shown in the diagram is a vertebrate, as it has a backbone; it is not a mammal (it's a reptile), not warm-blooded (reptiles are cold-blooded), and not a wasp (which is an insect).

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is not an elicit drug?
questions.explanation: Panadol is a legal, common pain relief medicine, unlike alcohol (legal but commonly abused), cocaine, and heroin, which are illicit drugs.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following correctly gives the age at which boys and girls reach puberty?
questions.explanation: Boys generally reach puberty around age 11, while girls reach puberty slightly earlier around age 10, reflecting the typical earlier onset of puberty in girls compared to boys.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
A learner described an animal as having the following features.
- moist skin
- cold blooded
- lives on land and in water
Which type of animal did the learner describe?
questions.explanation: Moist skin, cold-bloodedness, and living both on land and in water are characteristic features of amphibians.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which diseases are caused by tsetse flies in man and in cattle?

options.B: Man: Dysentery, Cattle: Foot and mouth disease

questions.explanation: Tsetse flies cause sleeping sickness in humans and nagana in cattle, both forms of trypanosomiasis transmitted by the same insect.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Why is lightning seen first before the thunder is heard?
questions.explanation: Light travels much faster than sound, so the flash of lightning is seen before the sound of thunder is heard, even though both occur simultaneously.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the following diagram.

(Diagram shows a tool with a wooden handle and metal end resting on a pivot)

What is the simple machine shown in the diagram?
questions.explanation: The tool shown, with a handle resting on a pivot/fulcrum to lift or move objects, is a lever.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following are ways of preventing the spread of HIV and AIDS?

options.A: Abstain from sex and avoid shaking hands.
options.B: Avoid drug abuse and HIV positive people.
options.C: Eat a balanced diet and sleep under a mosquito net.
options.D: Not sharing combs and razor blades.

questions.explanation: Not sharing combs and razor blades helps prevent HIV transmission through blood contact, unlike the other options which include irrelevant or unrelated actions (avoiding handshakes, avoiding HIV positive people entirely, sleeping under mosquito nets).

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the following first aid procedure.
1 Put the victim in an airy place
2 Use mouth to mouth resuscitation
3 Take the victim to a clinic or hospital
Which accident may prompt you to follow the laid down first aid procedure?
questions.explanation: Suffocation requires the described first aid procedure (fresh air, mouth-to-mouth resuscitation, medical attention) to restore breathing, unlike choking, electric shock, or snake bite which require different specific interventions.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: The movement of blood in the body is called ...
questions.explanation: The movement of blood throughout the body is called circulation.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the following diagram.

(Diagram shows a battery symbol with multiple cells)

The use of the above in a circuit is to ...

options.B: join the wires in a circuit.

questions.explanation: The battery (cells) shown in the diagram is used in a circuit to provide electric energy, powering the circuit.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Musical instruments and decorative objects are made from a combination of copper and zinc which is called ...
questions.explanation: Brass is an alloy made from a combination of copper and zinc, commonly used for musical instruments and decorative objects.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the following table showing metals and non-metals.

Metals: Aluminium, Carbon, Copper, Iron
Non-Metals: Hydrogen, Carbon dioxide, Oxygen, Zinc

Which of the following substances are placed in a wrong column?
questions.explanation: Carbon is actually a non-metal (placed incorrectly under Metals) and zinc is actually a metal (placed incorrectly under Non-Metals), so 'Carbon and zinc' are the substances placed in the wrong columns.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is not a health risk associated with early sexual debut?
questions.explanation: Stopping of the menstrual period is not a health risk associated with early sexual debut; cervical cancer, STIs/HIV, and teenage pregnancy are all known health risks linked to early sexual activity.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: During the test for starch, the brown colour indicates that there is...
questions.explanation: In the iodine starch test, a brown colour (the original colour of iodine solution) indicates the absence of starch, since starch would normally turn the solution blue/black.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following pairs of diseases affect the skin?
questions.explanation: Scabies and chicken pox are both diseases that affect the skin, unlike the other pairs which mix in diseases that affect other body systems (typhoid, bilharzia, flu).

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following wedges is used to cut firewood?
questions.explanation: An axe is a wedge-shaped tool specifically used to cut firewood, unlike a knife, nail, or screw which serve other purposes.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following consists of metals only?
questions.explanation: Iron, copper, and zinc are all metals, unlike the other options which include glass, plastic, or clay, which are non-metal materials.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: The process by which food is broken down into nutrients that body cells use is called ...
questions.explanation: Digestion is the process by which food is broken down into nutrients that the body's cells can use.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following methods of communication uses sound?
questions.explanation: A drum communicates through sound (beats), unlike email, fax, and letters which are written/visual forms of communication.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is a component of air?
questions.explanation: Oxygen is a gas and a component of air, unlike aluminium, carbon, and zinc which are solid elements/metals (carbon, while present as carbon dioxide gas, as a pure element is solid).

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is a component in an electric circuit?
questions.explanation: A connecting wire is a physical component used in an electric circuit, unlike current (which is a flow of charge, not a component), and a heater/pressing iron (which are appliances powered by a circuit, not components of it).

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: The volume of a liquid is measured using a ...
questions.explanation: A measuring cylinder is the correct instrument used to measure the volume of a liquid.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: The best way to conserve electricity is by ...

options.B: switching appliances off when not in use.
options.C: using electricity for cooking every time.

questions.explanation: Switching off appliances when not in use is the best way to conserve electricity, as it reduces unnecessary power consumption.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: When a person becomes dependent on a drug, it is said that the person is ...
questions.explanation: A person who becomes dependent on a drug is described as addicted.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: The function of the heart in the body is to ...
questions.explanation: The heart's function is to pump blood throughout the body.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the following diagram of a fish.

Which labelled part of the fish is used for swimming?

questions.question: ... are known to keep off bad smells in the pin-latrine.
questions.explanation: Ashes are traditionally used in pit-latrines to absorb moisture and keep off bad smells.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Explain why ingredients are weighed. To ...

options.B: discover the exact amount of something.
options.D: find out how heavy something is.

questions.explanation: Ingredients are weighed primarily to ensure accurate proportions, which leads to good/successful cooking results.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: The important reason for weighing and measuring ingredients is to ...

options.D: make sure all ingredients are available.

questions.explanation: The overarching purpose of accurately weighing and measuring ingredients is to achieve good results in the final cooked/baked product.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Give a reason why a measuring stone is put on a balance scale.

options.A: For ingredients to rest on a panel.
options.B: For the scale to work properly.
options.D: To know the weight of a stone and ingredients.

questions.explanation: A measuring/calibration stone of known weight is used on a balance scale to ensure the scale gives an accurate weight reading.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Describe the last two procedures for making a toy.
questions.explanation: In making a stuffed toy, the final steps are to put in the stuffing material and then close the opening to complete the toy.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Keeping of records in business will help one to know ...

options.A: losses only in his or her business.
options.B: profits only that he or she is making.
options.C: where to get goods for business.
options.D: whether there is progress or not in the business.

questions.explanation: Keeping business records allows the owner to track overall performance and determine whether the business is progressing (profit or loss), not just one aspect.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Part A shown on the diagram below is the ...
questions.explanation: Part A points to the gripping mechanism on the workbench used to hold/clamp wood securely, which is the bench vice.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which of the following is not a text format?
questions.explanation: Bold, Italic, and Normal are standard text formatting styles; 'Round' is not a recognised text format.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Why do people prefer phone calls to letter writing? Phone calls are ...
questions.explanation: Phone calls deliver messages instantly, making them faster than letter writing, which requires time for writing, posting, and delivery.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: What do we call drawings done by a builder before building a house?
questions.explanation: Drawings made by a builder before constructing a house are referred to as the plan.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Mwate was typing a letter of application for a job on her laptop. Her address was supposed to be aligned ... of the paper.
questions.explanation: In a standard formal/job application letter, the sender's address is conventionally aligned at the top right of the page.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: An example of a source of light in rural areas is ...
questions.explanation: A paraffin lamp is a common, traditional source of light used in rural areas without access to electricity.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: What is cursor status? It is ...

options.A: the colour of the cursor when typing.
options.B: the shape and position of the cursor when typing.
options.C: when the cursor is not used during typing.

questions.explanation: Cursor status refers to the shape and position of the cursor as it appears during typing on a computer.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: One of the pupils in grade 5 A was asked by his teacher to add words to the sign shown below. The words she added were ...
questions.explanation: The triangular warning sign showing people crossing represents a pedestrian crossing warning, so the appropriate caption is 'pedestrian crossing'.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Chanda designed the poster below. What type of lettering did he use?
questions.explanation: The poster text 'MAKE SAFE LOVE AIDS KILLS' is written entirely in capital letters, which is upper case lettering.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: The sign below was found on the side of the road. Which of the following words can be added to it to make it more meaningful?

options.C: The road has got lines on it.

questions.explanation: The triangular warning sign with a railway gate/crossing symbol warns drivers that there is a railway crossing ahead.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Mwanga drew the following objects. Which one of them represents an isometric drawing?

options.C: Larger 3D cube drawing with visible depth

questions.explanation: An isometric drawing shows a three-dimensional object with depth and equal angles between axes; the larger 3D cube drawing with visible depth best represents this.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: A pupil drew the symbol shown below and stuck it on her bag. It means ... this bag.
questions.explanation: A hand symbol crossed out with an 'X' is a universal warning symbol meaning 'do not touch'.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: After being typed, a document was being checked for possible miss spellings and missing words. The document was being ...
questions.explanation: Checking a typed document for spelling errors and missing words is the process of editing.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: To prevent hand tools from breaking in the workshop, Chawa put them in ...
questions.explanation: Storing hand tools properly in a tool box protects them from damage and breakage.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: To construct a radius of a circle below, the following points should be joined together by a straight line.
questions.explanation: A radius is a straight line joining the centre of a circle to a point on its circumference; point B is the centre and point A is on the circumference, so joining A and B forms a radius.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: To turn current on and off a ... is used.
questions.explanation: A switch is the device used to turn an electric current on and off.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Someone who discloses his/her ATM number or pin may...

options.A: have his/her money stolen from his/her account.
options.B: have his/her ATM card destroyed or stolen by the bank.
options.C: lose his/her code and the ATM card.

questions.explanation: Disclosing an ATM PIN/number to others creates a security risk, as it can allow unauthorised persons to access and steal money from the account.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which of the following tools is correctly paired with its use?
questions.explanation: A saw is correctly used for cutting wood; the other pairings (chisel-smoothening, hammer-holding, measuring tape-driving) are incorrect uses for those tools.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: The diagram below shows a teacher of woodwork demonstrating how to make a ... joint.
questions.explanation: The diagram shows a slot (mortice) being chiselled into one piece of wood to receive a matching peg (tenon) from another piece, which is the mortice and tenon joint.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: The letter P can be constructed under ... square boxes.
questions.explanation: The block letter 'P' is conventionally constructed within a 5×4 grid of square boxes in basic lettering/graphic design exercises.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Traditional dance is important in the history and culture of Zambia because it ...

options.A: helps to separate families in society.
options.B: places men to be superior over women folk.
options.C: passes on belief and history to future generations.

questions.explanation: Traditional dance serves as a way of passing on cultural beliefs and history from one generation to the next.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Why are swimmers asked to have a shower with soap before they can swim in a pool? It ...

options.A: helps to keep the swimming pool clean.
options.B: helps swimmers to be more flexible.

questions.explanation: Showering with soap before swimming removes dirt, sweat, and oils from the body, helping to keep the pool water clean.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a traditional basic equipment?
questions.explanation: A brazier is a traditional cooking/heating equipment used over charcoal, unlike the cooker, colander, and sink which are modern kitchen equipment.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Simutala sales groceries in a shop. Every day he records all his sales and items. He does this in order to know ...

options.B: how to be nice to customers.
options.D: whether he makes profit or loss.

questions.explanation: Recording daily sales and items helps a business owner track finances and determine whether the business is making a profit or a loss.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Mabvuto wanted to stretch a cold muscle but was stopped by his teacher. This was because ...

options.A: the muscle was going to become weak.
options.B: he was going to injure himself.
options.C: he could not maintain its shape.
options.D: he could not maintain its strength.

questions.explanation: Stretching a cold (unwarmed) muscle can cause injury such as strains or tears, which is why warming up is required before stretching.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Kamukape is a player who could run with a ball but only while bouncing it on the ground. The game is ...
questions.explanation: Dribbling (running while bouncing the ball) is a defining feature of basketball.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Shila performed a dance where she could follow specific steps and moves. This dance is called ... movement.
questions.explanation: A dance following specific, pre-set steps and moves is known as structured movement, as opposed to freestyle (improvised) movement.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: In which swimming race are participants allowed to use all the types of strokes?
questions.explanation: Freestyle swimming allows the swimmer to use any stroke of their choice, unlike backstroke and breaststroke which require a specific technique.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: The makishi dance is a cultural ceremony that is performed by ... speaking people.
questions.explanation: The Makishi masked dance ceremony is traditionally performed by the Luvale-speaking people of Zambia during initiation rites.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: The efficiency of the heart, the blood vessels and the lungs is called ...
questions.explanation: Cardiovascular fitness refers to how efficiently the heart, blood vessels, and lungs work together to supply oxygen to the body.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which craft technique uses paper, water and mixing flour to produce an artefact?
questions.explanation: Papier mache uses paper strips mixed with a flour-and-water paste to create craft artefacts.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Mumba always dresses nicely and decently. This means he looks ...
questions.explanation: Dressing nicely and decently gives a person a respectable appearance.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Colours that are opposite each other on the colour wheel are called ...
questions.explanation: Colours positioned directly opposite each other on the colour wheel are called complementary colours.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: To compose African music one should start with a ...
questions.explanation: In composing African music, the process typically begins with establishing a theme, followed by a tune, and then choreographing a dance to match.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: A picture made from collection of different pictures that are fitted together to make one picture is called ...
questions.explanation: A montage is created by combining multiple separate pictures/images together to form a single composite picture.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Enala mixed blue and green colours during a practical lesson. What type of colour did she make?
questions.explanation: Mixing a primary colour (blue) with an adjacent secondary colour (green) produces an intermediate (tertiary) colour; here it is classified as an intermediate colour.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: The five lines on which music is written are called ...
questions.explanation: The five horizontal lines on which musical notes are written are called the stave (or staff).

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Jane made it difficult for the opponent player in a volleyball game to return the ball by ...
questions.explanation: An overhead serve is hit with more power and speed, making it harder for the opponent to return the ball.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: In which of the gymnastic styles below is energy produced without oxygen being supplied to the muscles?
questions.explanation: Anaerobic means 'without oxygen'; anaerobic exercise produces energy without oxygen being supplied to the muscles.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: To produce good and strong clay bricks, John was advised to use clay from ...
questions.explanation: Clay found underground (subsoil) is denser and free of organic matter/roots, making it suitable for producing strong bricks.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Jane wants to take body measurements so that she can sew a dress. Which of the tools below is she going to use?
questions.explanation: A flexible tape measure (item iv) is the correct tool for taking body measurements, as it can bend around body curves unlike a rigid ruler.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: What is pool hygiene?

options.C: Making sure the water is flowing.
options.D: Painting the wall of the swimming pool blue.

questions.explanation: Pool hygiene refers to keeping the swimming pool clean and clear of dirt and contamination for safe use.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Applying safety rules help people ...
questions.explanation: Safety rules exist specifically to help prevent accidents and injuries.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Why should finger nails be kept short? To ...
questions.explanation: Keeping fingernails short helps prevent dirt and germs from accumulating underneath them, making it easier to keep them clean.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Describe one way to have good ventilation at night.

options.B: Do not over crowd in the room
options.D: Sleep with flowers in the room

questions.explanation: Avoiding overcrowding in a room at night allows for better air circulation and ventilation.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.topic: Home Economics - Health and Human Development
questions.question: Which of these statements describe the function of the skin?

options.B: Expose the body to air borne diseases.

questions.explanation: One of the main functions of the skin is to act as a protective barrier against germs and infections.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: What else do we use to unblock the kitchen sink apart from the suction pump?
questions.explanation: Hot water can help dissolve grease and clear blockages in a kitchen sink, serving as an alternative to a suction pump.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which of the following set has a correct list of measuring and weighing equipment?
questions.explanation: Cups and spoons are standard measuring tools used in cooking for both volume (cups) and small measures (spoons).

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Choose suitable foods which can be used to garnish a vegetable salad (cabbage salad).
questions.explanation: Hard boiled egg, cucumber, and tomato are commonly used as fresh, suitable garnishes for a cabbage/vegetable salad.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Luwa started with measuring ingredients before baking the cupcakes. He did that in order to ...
questions.explanation: Measuring ingredients accurately before baking ensures the correct proportions are used, making good use of ingredients for the best result.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Choose suitable foods which can be used to garnish a vegetable salad (cabbage salad).
questions.explanation: Hard boiled egg, cucumber, and tomato are suitable, fresh garnishes commonly used for a vegetable/cabbage salad.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Luwa started with measuring ingredients before baking the cupcakes. He did that in order to ...
questions.explanation: Measuring ingredients accurately ensures correct proportions, leading to good use of ingredients and a successful baking result.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: In the diagram below, identify the part marked Y.
questions.explanation: Part Y points to the base component into which the bulb is screwed, which is the bulb holder.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: It is advisable to wear appropriate clothing whenever working in a workshop so as to ...

options.A: be a role model to junior employees.
options.B: look presentable at place of work.

questions.explanation: Wearing appropriate clothing/protective wear in a workshop is primarily for personal safety and to enable proper work practices.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: When making wood or plastic items ... can be used to join the pieces.
questions.explanation: Glue is a suitable adhesive used to join pieces of wood or plastic together.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which of the following symbols compose parts of a simple electric circuit?
questions.explanation: A simple electric circuit requires a power source (cell/battery) and a load (bulb), which is represented in option B's symbol combination.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Doctors and nurses always wear masks and gloves whenever they are operating on a patient. This practice helps them avoid contracting ...
questions.explanation: Masks and gloves protect medical staff from contact with blood and bodily fluids, which can transmit HIV and AIDS during surgical procedures.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: First aid is the ...

options.A: attention given to a dead person.
options.B: initial help given to a casualty.
options.D: money given to an injured persons relatives.

questions.explanation: First aid refers to the immediate, initial assistance given to an injured person (casualty) before professional medical help arrives.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Who is an entrepreneur? One who ...

options.B: runs another person's business without getting paid.
options.C: takes an idea and uses it to make money using required skills and resources.
options.D: runs a shop on behalf of another person who lives abroad.

questions.explanation: An entrepreneur is someone who takes an idea and uses required skills and resources to generate income/profit from it.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: A pupil used a copper wire, a bulb and metal paper clip to ...
questions.explanation: Copper wire (conductor), a bulb (load), and a metal paper clip (can act as a switch) are the basic components for constructing a simple circuit.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: A customer at an Automated Teller Machine (ATM) was advised to move back when another customer was withdrawing money. This was to ...

options.A: let him see the ATM card number of the other customer.
options.B: prevent him from seeing the pin number of the other customer.
options.C: prevent the ATM from retaining the card.
options.D: protect the ATM from being damaged.

questions.explanation: Standing back at an ATM protects another customer's privacy by preventing onlookers from seeing their PIN number, which helps prevent fraud.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Posters are widely used to communicate messages because they ...
questions.explanation: Posters are effective communication tools because they can visually depict different situations and messages clearly to an audience.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Lukonde made a switch to a simple circuit using a ...

options.B: piece of wood and a rubber.

questions.explanation: A metal paper clip conducts electricity and can function as a switch in a simple circuit, unlike plastic paper clips, wood, or rubber which are insulators.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Before a builder starts building, he will first draw the ... of the building he intends to build.
questions.explanation: Before construction begins, a builder first draws the plan of the building, which is the basic blueprint/layout.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Nyirenda used a ... calculator to perform other operations apart from addition, subtraction, division and multiplication.
questions.explanation: A scientific calculator has additional functions beyond the basic four operations, such as trigonometric, logarithmic, and exponential calculations.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: How can we prevent metal tools from rusting? By ...
questions.explanation: Applying oil to metal tools creates a protective barrier against moisture and air, preventing rust formation.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which of the following correctly pairs the tools and their use?
questions.explanation: Steel rule and try square are correctly paired as measuring tools, used for taking and checking measurements and right angles.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Manipulating a graphic image involves three of the following except ...
questions.explanation: Changing colour, deleting, and resizing are all valid graphic manipulation actions; 'filing colour' is not a recognised image manipulation term.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: What is text alignment in computer studies? It is ...

options.B: positioning a text in a straight line.
options.C: reducing the size and font of the text.
options.D: underlining and reducing the font of the text.

questions.explanation: Text alignment refers to positioning text in a straight line relative to the page margins (e.g. left, right, centre, justified).

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: A teacher of a grade 5 class demonstrated to her class how to bisect a 90° angle using a ruler, a pencil and a ...

options.A: pair of compasses and set square.

questions.explanation: Bisecting an angle accurately requires a protractor (to measure/draw the angle) and a pair of compasses (to construct the bisector).

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: To produce a three sided object, one would use ... projection.
questions.explanation: Isometric projection is used to represent three-dimensional objects, showing three sides/faces in a single 2D drawing.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which of the following is a text format?
questions.explanation: Bold is a text format/style applied to characters, whereas alignment, centre, and right are types of text alignment, not formats themselves.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question:
The musical scale below is incomplete, which note is missing?
doh, re, mi, fa, so, ... ti, doh.
questions.explanation: The complete diatonic solfa scale is doh, re, mi, fa, so, la, ti, doh. The missing note between 'so' and 'ti' is 'la'.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which set shows primary colours?
questions.explanation: The primary colours are blue, red and yellow; all other colours are made by mixing these.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: A farmer bought a hoe, after a few days the handle was eaten up. The maker did not ... it.
questions.explanation: Wood handles are treated with varnish to protect them from insects/termites; without varnishing (here written 'vanish'), the wood gets eaten.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which of the following is not needed when making letter stencils?
questions.explanation: Letter stencils are typically cut out from paper using scissors and traced/coloured with pencils; water colours are not a standard requirement for making the stencil itself.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: A semi quaver represents ... of a crotchet.
questions.explanation: A semi-quaver (sixteenth note) is one quarter the duration of a crotchet (quarter note), since a crotchet = 4 semi-quavers.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: The musical note shown below is a ...
questions.explanation: A note with a single flag on its stem is a quaver (eighth note).

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Nyau dancers are practised by the ... people of Zambia.
questions.explanation: The Nyau (Gule Wamkulu) masked dance tradition is associated with the Chewa people.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Drum beat creates ...
questions.explanation: Drum beats provide the rhythmic pulse in music, i.e. rhythm.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question:
Which method is best for saving a drowning person?
1: Swim and grab the person with hands
2: Reach the person with a stick or branch
3: Wait for elders to come
4: Swim and tow
questions.explanation: Reaching a drowning person with a stick or branch (method 2) is the safest rescue method, as it avoids the rescuer being pulled under by the panicking drowning person.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: A learner pulled the rope tied to the block as shown below. This is an example of...
questions.explanation: Pulling against an immovable, fixed block involves muscle contraction without movement/change in muscle length, which is an isometric exercise.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: The Learners were asked to perform different jumping skills. The diagram shows ...jump.
questions.explanation: The scissors jump technique in high jump involves a leg kicking motion as shown, where the legs scissor over the bar one after another.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question:
The diagram below represents a colour wheel.
Which of the two colours when combined will produce a tertiary colour?
questions.explanation: A tertiary colour is formed by mixing a primary colour with an adjacent secondary colour on the colour wheel; positions 2 and 4 correspond to such adjacent primary-secondary pairing on the wheel shown.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: In imaginative drawings, the first step is to ... the object.
questions.explanation: In drawing, the first step is always to sketch an outline of the object before painting or shading.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Bleeding from a cut on the finger can be stopped by putting on ...
questions.explanation: An adhesive plaster (band-aid/sticking plaster) is the correct first-aid item used to cover and stop bleeding from a small finger cut.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Learners sang and played musical instruments. This is called ... music.
questions.explanation: Music performed in real time by singers and instrumentalists (not played back from a recording) is called live music.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which ethnic group is associated with the musical instrument shown below?
questions.explanation: The plucked metal-key instrument (mbira-type) shown is traditionally associated with the Kaonde people of Zambia.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Using basic skills of floating, kicking legs and alternating hands in swimming is called ... stroke.
questions.explanation: The crawl stroke involves floating, kicking the legs, and alternating arm/hand movements.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which sporting activity is associated with instrument below?
questions.explanation: The discus shown in the picture is thrown in a field event (discus throw), which falls under field events in athletics.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: What would a swimmer wear to keep the hair away from the face while swimming?
questions.explanation: A swimming cap is worn to keep hair away from the face and out of the water while swimming.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Grade 7 Learners were asked by the teacher to make a basket. Which method would not be used?
questions.explanation: Baskets are traditionally made using braiding, plaiting, or weaving techniques with reeds/fibres; knitting uses yarn and needles and is not used for basket-making.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which of these symbols mean, "do not wash"?

options.C: Symbol C (washtub with diagonal line and dot)
options.D: Symbol D (washtub with cross and wavy line)

questions.explanation: The laundry symbol showing a washtub crossed out completely with an 'X' means 'do not wash'.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.topic: Home Economics - Health and Human Development
questions.question: During puberty ... changes in boys.
questions.explanation: A key physical change during puberty in boys is the deepening/breaking of the voice due to the growth of the voice box (larynx).

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: The best and cheap method of making water safe to drink is ...
questions.explanation: Boiling water is widely regarded as the cheapest and most effective method available to households for making water safe to drink by killing pathogens.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which crop is not suitable to grow in the kitchen garden?
questions.explanation: Maize requires a lot of space and is grown as a field crop, unlike cabbage, onion, and tomato which are typical kitchen garden vegetables.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: In the weekly cleaning of the bedroom. What step should come first?
questions.explanation: Opening the windows first allows fresh air circulation and lets dust settle/escape before further cleaning steps are carried out.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: The instrument below is used to protect the middle finger when sewing? Name the instrument.
questions.explanation: A thimble is worn on the finger to protect it from being pricked by the needle while sewing.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which household pest contaminates food?
questions.explanation: Flies land on waste and dirt and then on food, transferring germs and contaminating it.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: An entrepreneur is a person who starts a business hoping to ...
questions.explanation: An entrepreneur starts a business with the aim of making a profit through providing goods or services.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: How can you preserve beans and pumpkins leaves?
questions.explanation: Beans and pumpkin leaves are traditionally preserved by drying, which removes moisture and prevents spoilage.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: A colander is a kitchen utensil used for ...
questions.explanation: A colander has holes/perforations used to drain liquid from food such as washed vegetables or cooked pasta.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: The stitch shown on the piece of cloth is a ... stitch.

passages.subject: English
passages.title: Story 1: The Pineapple
passages.text:
A good juicy and ripe pineapple can satisfy craving just as any chocolate bar. In addition to being a delicious food, there are many health properties in the fruit. It is grown abundantly in North Western Province of Zambia, especially around Ikelenge. The best can be achieved through eating it fresh, canned or frozen or by drinking its juice.

The Pineapple is loaded with vitamins and minerals. Its nutrients include calcium, potassium, fibre and vitamin C. Besides, it is low in fat and cholesterol. It is high in the enzyme bromelain and the antioxidant vitamin C, both of which play a major role in the body's healing process.

passages.subject: English
passages.title: Story 2: Trade and the Triangular Slave Trade
passages.text:
Trade is an activity that has been practiced by the human race since time immemorial and reached its peak in the 18th Century. Initially, this trade was done in a primitive manner because quantity and quality was not considered. Trade therefore, is the buying and selling of goods and services. The form of trade and the way it is carried out has constantly changed and improved with to time.

In Africa and Zambia in particular, trade traces its roots from the era of the slave trade. The development of the manufacturing industry in Europe led to the demand for raw materials and labour to work on the plantations in America. Africa was seen as the source of both cheap labour and raw materials. This marked the beginning of the slave trade, and it continued for many years. The slaves were expected to work extra hard on the plantations.

Farming activities on the plantations increased because of the demand for raw materials. Because of the nature of the trade, it formed a pattern which became known as the "Triangular Slave Trade".

passages.subject: English
passages.title: Story 3: The Scout Movement
passages.text: We have seen that Scouting is a Movement for young people, with its emphasis on camping, the out doors and all those activities which are attractive to boys and girls, but so are many other youth organisations. Scouting is however, unique. What makes it so is something called "ethos" of Scouting. "Ethos" is a Greek word which means the "Spirit." The essence of this spirit is found in the Scout Promise and the Law. Most of the young people who enter the Scout Movement are attracted by the outdoor life, the camping, but hardly the spirit as such. It is the responsibility of Scout leaders to guide the young people, so leaders should know what it is all about.

passages.subject: English
passages.title: Text 2: The Cemetery Story
passages.text:
Two little boys stole a big bag of oranges from a church orchard and decided to go to a quiet place to share them equally. One of them suggested they do it from a nearby cemetery. Two oranges dropped out of the big bag as they were climbing over the cemetery gate but they did not bother to pick them up since they had enough in the bag.

Few minutes later, a drunkard on his way from a bar passed near the cemetery gate and heard a voice saying, "One for me, one for you, one for me, one for you."

He immediately sobered up and ran as fast as he could to a nearby church to report his mystery to the priest. When he arrived at the church, he said to the priest, "Father, please come with me! Come and witness ghosts sharing corpses at the cemetery!"

They both rushed to the cemetery gate and the voice continued, "One for me, one for you, one for me, one for you."

Suddenly, the voice stopped counting and said, "What about the two at the gate?" The two men at the gate ran away shouting, "We are not dead yet! Please spare us!"

passages.subject: English
passages.title: Text 3: The Life Cycle of a Butterfly
passages.text:
The life cycle of a butterfly is an amazing process in nature. In just a few simple steps, a short, stubby caterpillar develops into a beautiful multi-coloured butterfly.

The cycle begins when an adult butterfly lays a handful of tiny round eggs onto a leaf. Compared to humans raising their young, butterflies have such an easier job. They simply find the kind of plant their future offspring will be able to eat, lay their eggs onto it, then go on their way. When one of these eggs hatches, a larva emerges.

When the larva emerges, it starts its life by eating the leaf it was born onto. It is critical that the mother butterfly lays her eggs on the type of leaf the larva will like to eat.

Following the larva stage is the pupa stage. The pupa stage is one of the most magnificent stages of a butterfly's life. As soon as a larva is done growing and has reached its full length and weight, it forms itself into a pupa.

Finally, when the larva has done all of its forming the adult butterfly emerges.

passages.subject: English
passages.title: THE SECRET OF LONG LIFE
passages.text:
Munene had just celebrated his 114th birthday. Reporters visited him in his remote village in Shangombo, Western Province to find out the secret of long life.

"The secret of long life," Munene said, "is happiness. If you are happy, you will live a long time."

"Are you married?" a reporter asked.

"Yes," Munene replied. "I married my third wife when I was 102 years old. If you are happily married, you live forever. If it had not been for my third wife I would have died years ago."

"What about smoking and drinking?" a reporter asked.

"Yes, they are important", Munene said. "Don't smoke at all and you will feel well. Drink two glasses of wine a day and you will be healthy and happy."

"If you could live your life again, what would you do?" the reporter asked.

"I would do what I have done. If I had had more sense, I would eat more yoghourt!" he laughed.

"Supposing you could change one thing in your life, what would you change?" another reporter asked.

"Not much," Munene replied.

"So you don't have any regrets?"

"Yes, I have one regret," Munene replied. "If I had known that I was going to live so long, I would have looked after myself better!".

passages.subject: English
passages.title: Text 3: The Pencil
passages.text:
A pencil is an interesting object to look at. One may wonder what this really is and how it is made. It has a number of characteristics and can be described in many ways. It is cheap, legible and light in weight. A pencil is easy to carry around and unlike a ball pen; it never leaks.

A pencil is one of the most widely used writing instrument in the world. The pencil was discovered 1,500 years ago in England. The writing material had a variety of names such as lead, wad and plumbage meaning, that which acts like lead. No one knew exactly what this thing really was until in 1979 when a Swedish chemist, Carl Scheele made the surprising discovery that black lead was not lead at all but a soft form of pure carbon.

It is made of a solution of finely ground graphite, clay and water. This is forced through a narrow metal tube and comes out looking like a long string of spaghetti which is dried, cut and fried in a kiln. The lead is then immersed in hot oil and wax. Then cedar wood is used because it is easy to sharpen.

Today, we have a variety of pencil types such as 'HB' and 'B' which denotes softer lead. It is usually numbered 2B or 6B to show how soft the material is.

passages.subject: English
passages.title: Text 2: Sipho
passages.text:
When Sipho came to my class, he was nearly seven years old. Within two weeks of starting school, he was already proving to be difficult in class. He was playful, often teased other children and disturbed them in class. He refused to write anything and always lost his pencils.

One day, he was sitting on the carpet with the rest of the class. I asked some children in the class to tell me their news. Sipho wanted to talk but I did not let him have a turn that day. At the end of the session, everybody had to go back to their desks and draw a picture about some news. Sipho refused to return to his desk.

When I repeated my instruction, he pretended that he did not understand me. When I went over to him to lead him back to his seat, he ran away, racing through the class laughing at me. I got very angry and told him to be back in his seat by the count of three. I counted to three but he was still running around laughing.

Although I was angry, I did not want to hit him. However, I felt degraded at the thought of chasing Sipho in front of all other children. All these feelings made me react in a personal way. The next time Sipho came close to me, I grabbed him and pushed him out of the classroom. Once Sipho was outside, I closed the door. He made a lot of noise such that everybody in the school knew what was going on.

Eventually, the head teacher came around and asked what had happened. She then took Sipho to her office and spoke to him, trying to find out why he was so troublesome. In the end, Sipho had to apologise to me before he was allowed back into my class. I was so grateful that the head teacher was so supportive, but deep inside I felt like a failure.

passages.subject: English
passages.title: Text 3: Moths
passages.text:
Moths are not liked by many people because they look ugly. When people light their rooms up at night, moths become a great nuisance as they happily swarm around the roof. If one catches them, they leave a powderly substance on the person's palms. This substance comes from their wings.

If a family uses a candle, a lamp or any other homemade lighting system, moths usually put them out, leaving the family in darkness. To some people, moths are supposed to be killed at once, even to an extent of using insecticide. Early in the morning, moths are so weakened by the cold that they cannot move.

People who keep chickens invite them in to feed on the moths. But how many people know that moths are useful to them?

When moths lay their eggs on tree branches and twigs, they hatch into caterpillars. Caterpillars are a protein-giving food and are widely eaten in many parts of Zambia. Caterpillars are of different types. People should therefore learn to like and preserve moths as they are a source of food.

passages.subject: English
passages.title: Text 2: Puberty
passages.text:
Children often learn about the changes at puberty and sex from their peers. They hear myths that may confuse them and lead to risky behaviour. It is important that children are taught about body changes before these changes take place to avoid anxieties, teen pregnancies and risky behaviour.

There are several physical changes that take place in boys and girls during puberty. These changes differ from child to child. Some experience these changes as early as 9 years old, while in others, these may delay up to the age of 14. Regardless when puberty occurs, there is need to educate children nearing their adolescence about puberty and expected behaviour.

Physical changes in boys are different from those in girls. In general, girls experience rapid physical growth and often grow taller than boys of the same age. Their hips widen, pubic hair appears, their breasts begin to develop and their menstrual periods start. In boys, sex organs, pubic hair and beards grow. They also begin to have big appetites and their voices deepen. With all these developments, it is therefore important to be prepared mentally for such changes.

passages.subject: English
passages.title: Text 3: Sipho and Smoking
passages.text:
One day when Sipho was at school, he saw one of his friends smoking something. It looked like tobacco. So, Sipho asked his friend to give him one cigarette so that he could feel the way people who smoke tobacco feel. His friend gave him one cigarette. He took a puff and started coughing. After a few minutes, Sipho started running around the school shouting and chasing other learners. Then, he started to feel dizzy and sick.

The teacher came in and asked him what was wrong. He could not tell him because he was feeling dizzy and was panting. The teacher took Sipho to the clinic. At the clinic the nurse examined him and told the teacher that he had smoked dagga. The following day, he felt better. He told himself never to smoke tobacco nor dagga because it made him feel sick.

passages.subject: English
passages.title: Text 2: The Invention of Paper
passages.text:
The ancient Chinese invented paper about 2,000 years ago. Chinese papermakers used fibre from tree barks and old rags to make paper. The art of paper making spread from China to other parts of the world about 500 years later. It finally reached Europe around 900 years ago.

The invention of the printing press in the 1400s made books popular, and the demand for paper increased. All paper was handmade until 1798. That is when a Frenchman named Nicholas Robert invented a paper-making machine that could make paper in continuous rolls.

Before the invention of paper, ancient people used many different surfaces for writing. They wrote on clay, wood, stone and metal. More than 4,500 years ago, the ancient Egyptians made a paper like material from a plant called papyrus. This material is used to make soft reed mats in Zambia today. Papyrus reeds were cut into flat slices, layered, soaked in water and later pressed into sheets. The English word 'paper', comes from the word 'papyrus'.

passages.subject: English
passages.title: Text 3: The King of the Forest and Kalulu
passages.text:
Once upon a time, a big lion, the King of the Forest decided that all old and powerless animals must be put to death so that no useless animal occupied the space and ate the food reserved for the young and strong ones. Following this cruel decision, all the old animals were killed mercilessly, but one clever old hare, called Kalulu, hid himself in a cave and survived this killing. One day, the big lion was sleeping. He had kept his mouth open as he had a bad cold. A long snake passing by mistook the lion's open mouth for a hole. It entered and made itself comfortable in the lion's throat. The lion suffered from terrible pain.

Upon seeing this, the other animals felt pity for the lion and tried everything possible to help the lion but to no avail. The surviving old Kalulu heard the sad news. He made a plan and came to the lion's rescue. He used a rat to make the snake come out in order to catch the rat. In this way, the lion was saved. The King of the Forest thanked the old Kalulu and realised that old animals are important. He announced that no old animal should be killed any more. From that time, all animals lived happily.
